==Attempts to rejoin rugby union==
===Quits rugby league and returns to Moawhango===
It was reported in April 1939 that "Manukau has missed the brilliance of R. Chase, who is expected back in Auckland next Saturday".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Manukau's Form/Forwards Excel
| page = 22
| publisher = New Zealand Herald, Volume LXXVI, Issue 23330
| date = 26 April 1938
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/NZH19390426.2.180.3
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> He did not however return to [[Auckland]]. At the end of the season it was mentioned in the Auckland Star that Gregory from [[Rotorua]] had "filled the big gap caused by R. Chase dropping out of the game".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Roope Rooster/Manukau In Form
| page = 12
| publisher = Auckland Star
| date = 4 September 1938
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/AS19390904.2.113
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

===Application for reinstatement to rugby union===
On 13 June 1939 it was reported that Chase wished to be reinstated into rugby union. The [[Wanganui Chronicle]] stated "Rangi Chase, the well-known New Zealand representative league player wants to be reinstated to rugby according to information which Mr. W. Pine, a member of the Taihape Rugby Union, supplied to the Union at a meeting of the management committee… "Rangi Chase is now a married man living in the Taihape district and he informs me that he wishes to be reinstated, and has no intention of playing league again", Mr. Pine said. "Circumstances have changed since Rangi last applied for reinstatement and then played league. He was a single man then and was persuaded to return to Auckland. He is now married and has given me a definite assurance that he intends to remain in the district and desires to don the rugby jersey again. If Rangi Chase returned to league this season he would be a certainty for the trip to [[England]], but his is emphatic in his assertion that he has no desire to play league against… "will the union consider his supplication" inquired Mr. Pine. The chairman, Mr. J. Webb, pointed out that the matter rested with the Whanganui Rugby Union. If Chase put in his application in writing to the Taihape Union it would be forwarded on to Wanganui, added Mr. Webb. Other members agreed with the views expressed by the chairman, and it was pointed out that Chase would be a great asset to rugby if he was reinstated. It was left to Mr. Pine to advice Chase to put in his application at once so that it could be sent to Whanganui without delay".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Rangi Chase/Wants To Be Reinstated
| page = 4
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 83, Issue 139
| date = 15 June 1939
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19390615.2.12
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> On 6 July it was reported that his application had been handed in to the Taihape Rugby Union which has forwarded it on to the Wanganui Union.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Application for Reinstatement
| page = 10
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 83, Issue 157
| date = 6 July 1939
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19390706.2.92.10
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> The application was clearly refused because 2 years later in 1941 Chase was once again applying for reinstatement.

It was reported in February 1941 that he and a companion saved two lost girls in the Whakatara Bush. The girls had left home at [[Moawhango]] at 6 o'clock on Tuesday (11 February), heading to their home at Turangaarere and spent the night out in the open. They were found on Wednesday afternoon by "Messrs. A. Payne, and Rangi Chase, the well-known rugby league footballer".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Maori Girls Lost And Found/Spent Night In Open
| page = 4
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 38
| date = 14 February 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410214.2.40
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

In early April 1941 the Whanganui Rugby Union met and decided to forward the name of Chase to the [[New Zealand Rugby|New Zealand Rugby Union]] once more for reinstatement to the rugby code. The Wanganui Chronicle said he "was now residing permanently at [[Moawhango]]". The application "was supported by the Taihape delegates and Mr. A. Takarangi".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Wanganui Rugby Union Control/Annual Meeting Held
| page =7
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 80
| date = 4 April 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410404.2.89
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> It was also reported that he had been reinstated once before but returned to league, however he did not actually play a game of rugby union at the time. This was most likely at the end of the 1937 season prior to returning to Manukau for the 1938 season.<ref>{{cite news
| title = General Sports News
| page = 3
| publisher = New Zealand Herald, Volume LXXVIII, Issue 23941
| date = 16 April 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/NZH19410416.2.19
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> The New Zealand Rugby Union advised in June "that the application for reinstatement of Rangi Chase (Taihape) had been considered and declined".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Wanganui Rugby Union/Fortnightly Meeting
| page = 6
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 135
| date = 11 June 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410611.2.66
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

It appears however that Chase did play in a few matches in 1941. A match was played on 28 June at the Oval Domain in Taihape between the West and East in the Taihape district. The match was of a charitable nature and not part of a formal union competition. Each side was representing the eastern and western queen candidates in the patriotic carnival. The East side, which Chase was part of won the match 17 to 6. The Wanganui Chronicle reported that "R. Chase showed glimpses" of his old form along with some other experienced players.<ref>{{cite news
| title = News From Taihape/Rugby Match
| page = 3
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 153
| date = 2 July 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410702.2.13
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> Then on 5 July he played again for the East against the West in a second patriotic carnival match. The East won once again by 9 points to 6. Chase was listed in amongst the most "outstanding players" for the East team.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Taihape Notes/Rugby Football/East Defeats West
| page = 3
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 157
| date = 8 July 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410708.2.13
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> A further match was played between the East team and a Taihape Town team but it is unknown if Chase played. The on 2 August a Taihape representative side played against the Waiouru Military Camp at Taihape with Chase named in the team in the threequarters.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Rugby Football
| page = 3
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 176
| date = 29 July 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410729.2.10
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> Taihape in maroon, won the match by 8 points to 5 with Chase impressing at times.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Taihape News/Rugby Football/Taihape v. Waiouru
| page = 7
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 85, Issue 182
| date = 5 August 1941
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19410805.2.79
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

In May 1944 it was reported that Chase had once again applied for reinstatement into rugby union with his application received by the Whanganui Rugby Union from the Taihape Union. It was left "in the hands of the chairman to approach the authorities concerned".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Wanganui Rugby/Team Managers For Saturday
| page = 3
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle, Volume 88, Issue 122
| date = 24 May 1944
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19440524.2.14
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

===Wanganui Māori league game===
It appears once again that Chase's application to play rugby union was turned down as in 1946 he was named to play for Wanganui Māori rugby league team against a Wanganui [[Pākehā]] rugby league side. Remarkably he was still only aged 27 but had barely played any rugby or league in 7 years. The match was played at Cooks Garden in Wanganui and was a curtain-raiser to the [[Wellington rugby league team|Wellington]] – [[Taranaki rugby league team|Taranaki]] match. In the main match Chase's old teammate [[Jack Hemi]] was playing for Wellington after moving there not long earlier. Chase was listed in the threequarters for the Māori side along with J. Waitai, and D. Downs. The Māori side wore red and green while the Pakeha side wore blue and white.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Maori v. Pakeha
| page = 2
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 2 August 1946
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19460802.2.4.14
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> Chase's Māori side won the match by 11 points to 5. The game was described as a "fast and spectacular" throughout with Chase scoring an unconverted try in the first half.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Maori v. Pakeha
| page = 3
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 5 August 1946
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19460805.2.21
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

Then in July 1947 Chase applied yet again for reinstatement into rugby union but was declined again.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Wanganui Rugby/Management Committee
| page = 9
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 10 July 1947
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19470710.2.90.2
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> Later in the year on 20 September Chase did however play for Taihape Māori against Taihape Pākehā.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Taihape Notes
| page = 2
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 17 September 1947
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19470917.2.6
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> The match was played at the Taihape Recreation Ground with the Pākehā side winning 17 to 11. In the first half chase converted a try, and then kicked a penalty which gave the Māori side an 11–9 lead.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Rugby Season Ended
| page = 2
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 22 September 1947
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19470922.2.5
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Rangi married Mariana Chase (née Iwikau) and they had 8 children, Gary Hiraka Chase (b. 1941), Lyall, Rich, Graeme (Snow), Pop, Teresa, and Bill. Chase's father, Henare (Harry), died on 7 December 1948. At the Taihape sub union meeting in early March of the following year those present stood in silence in respect to his memory. He was noted as "a rugby stalwart, administrator and prominent former player".<ref>{{cite news
| title = Personal
| page = 7
| publisher = Wanganui Chronicle
| date = 25 March 1949
| url =https://paperspast.natlib.govt.nz/newspapers/WC19490325.2.78
| access-date = 12 August 2022}}</ref> His aunt, Te Reiti Grace was interviewed in 1983, when aged 91 about her life.<ref>{{cite news
| title = Mrs Te Reiti Grace Interview
| publisher = Ngā Taonga/Sound & Vision
| url =https://www.ngataonga.org.nz/collections/catalogue/catalogue-item?record_id=224117
| access-date = 26 July 2022}}</ref> Rangi died on September 7, 1998, aged 79 in Moawhango. His wife, Mariana, died in Taihape on 20 December 2011, aged 93.
 
One of Rangi's great-granddaughters is [[Silver Fern]], [[Liana Leota]] ([[née]] '''Barrett-Chase'''; born 31 October 1984). While his sister, Ginny Thompson [[née]] Chase is the great-grandmother of [[New Zealand national rugby union team|All Black]], [[Damian McKenzie]]. Rangi's grand-niece, Michele Chase married Paul Stanley and they had a son [[Chase Stanley]] who played rugby league for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] while another son, [[Kyle Stanley (rugby league)|Kyle Stanley]] played rugby league for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]].{{citation needed|date=July 2025}}
